prerequisite-env-vars.for-gcp: PULUMI_ACCESS_TOKEN, PULUMI_CONFIG_PASSPHRASE, HF_TOKEN, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI (= MLFLOW_GCP_URI = Cloud Run URL), MLFLOW_GCP_URI (= Cloud Run URL from Pulumi output), MLFLOW_ENABLE_PROXY_MULTIPART_UPLOAD=true, MLFLOW_MULTIPART_UPLOAD_CHUNK_SIZE=10485760
prerequisite-env-vars.for-runpod: RUNPOD_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN (for GHCR private image pull), HF_TOKEN (for VesselFM weights only)
prerequisite-env-vars.explicitly-banned-from-gcp-yaml: DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL, DVC_S3_ACCESS_KEY, DVC_S3_SECRET_KEY, DVC_S3_BUCKET, MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI, MLFLOW_CLOUD_USERNAME, MLFLOW_CLOUD_PASSWORD

done.description: Docker image built + pushed to GAR
Pulumi stack deployed — Cloud SQL, Cloud Run MLflow, GCS, GAR all UP
Cloud Run MLflow responding at MLFLOW_GCP_URI

task.name: Verify GCP Cloud Run MLflow is healthy

commands.cmd: curl -s "${MLFLOW_GCP_URI}/health" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('MLflow OK:', d)"

task.acceptance: HTTP 200 with status OK from Cloud Run MLflow.
task.on-failure: If Cloud Run is down: cd deployment/pulumi/gcp && pulumi up -y If URL is wrong: export MLFLOW_GCP_URI=$(cd deployment/pulumi/gcp && pulumi stack output mlflow_url)

task.name: Verify GCS DVC bucket is populated
task.description: CORRECTED from v2 T0.5.5 (which checked UpCloud S3 — WRONG for GCP). GCP data source = GCS bucket gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data via ADC. DVC must be configured with gcs remote pointing to this bucket.

commands.cmd: # Expected: at least 350 files (minivess dataset) # If bucket is EMPTY, run one-time data push from local: # dvc push -r gcs # Requires local gcs DVC remote: dvc remote add gcs gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data # Requires ADC: gcloud auth application-default login

task.acceptance: gsutil ls shows ≥350 objects. Bucket is non-empty.
task.on-failure: If bucket empty: 1. gcloud auth application-default login # ADC for local machine 2. dvc remote add gcs gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data # in .dvc/config 3. dvc push -r gcs # one-time push (may take 20-30 min for 2.7 GB) Note: This is a ONE-TIME operation. Never needed again after initial push.

task.name: Verify GAR image exists

commands.cmd: GAR_REPO=$(cd deployment/pulumi/gcp && pulumi stack output docker_registry 2>/dev/null || echo "europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess")

gcloud artifacts docker images list ${GAR_REPO} --limit=3 2>/dev/null | grep base || \ docker manifest inspect ${GAR_REPO}/base:latest 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print('GAR image OK')" || \ echo "WARNING: GAR image not found — run make push-gar to push"

task.name: Verify SkyPilot GCP and RunPod enabled

commands.cmd: uv run sky check gcp 2>&1 | grep -E "(GCP|enabled|disabled)"
uv run sky check runpod 2>&1 | grep -E "(RunPod|enabled|disabled)"
uv run sky status 2>&1 | head -10 # Should show no running clusters

task.acceptance: GCP: enabled. RunPod: enabled. No orphan clusters.

task.name: Verify .env contains correct vars (no banned vars in use)

commands.cmd:
python3 - <<'PYEOF' import os from pathlib import Path
env_content = Path(".env").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
required_gcp = ["MLFLOW_GCP_URI", "MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI", "HF_TOKEN"] required_runpod = ["RUNPOD_API_KEY", "GITHUB_TOKEN"] banned_for_cloud = [] # These should NOT be passed to GCP/RunPod SkyPilot YAMLs
print("=== Required GCP vars ===") for var in required_gcp: val = os.environ.get(var, "") status = "OK" if val else "MISSING" print(f" {status}: {var}")
print("=== Required RunPod vars ===") for var in required_runpod: val = os.environ.get(var, "") status = "OK" if val else "MISSING" print(f" {status}: {var}")
# Verify smoke_test_gcp.yaml does NOT reference banned S3 vars gcp_yaml = Path("deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gcp.yaml").read_text(encoding="utf-8") banned = ["DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL", "DVC_S3_ACCESS_KEY", "DVC_S3_SECRET_KEY", "MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI"] print("=== GCP YAML sanity check ===") for b in banned: if b in gcp_yaml: print(f" FAIL: {b} found in smoke_test_gcp.yaml — MUST be removed!") else: print(f" OK: {b} absent from smoke_test_gcp.yaml") PYEOF

task.acceptance: All required vars OK. Zero FAIL lines from YAML sanity check.
task.on-failure: If smoke_test_gcp.yaml still has DVC_S3_* vars: apply fix from Phase 1 (T1.1). If MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI missing from .env: it should equal MLFLOW_GCP_URI value.

phase.name: Fix smoke_test_gcp.yaml — GCS Data Source
phase.description:
The smoke_test_gcp.yaml was reverted to the old UpCloud S3 version during the QA session. This phase applies the correct GCS architecture. The fix was previously committed as part of the session but may need re-applying on this branch.
ARCHITECTURE (GCP): Data: GCS bucket gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data via ADC (NO S3 credentials) MLflow: Cloud Run at ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI} (must equal MLFLOW_GCP_URI) Image: GAR europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest GPU: L4 only (T4 BANNED — no BF16, NaN in SAM3 encoder)
ARCHITECTURE (RunPod — smoke_test_gpu.yaml, ALREADY CORRECT): Data: Network Volume /opt/vol/data/raw/ → symlink to /app/data/raw/minivess Fallback: s3://minivessdataset (AWS public, no credentials) MLflow: File-based /opt/vol/mlruns (NO remote server) Image: GHCR ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:latest GPU: RTX4090/RTX5090/RTX3090

task.name: Verify and fix smoke_test_gcp.yaml content
task.description: Read the file and check if DVC_S3_* vars are present. If yes: replace with GCS setup. If already correct: skip. NEVER use sed/awk (CLAUDE.md Rule #16). Use Python pathlib + str.replace() for targeted edits.
task.expected-envs-block: MODEL_FAMILY: sam3_vanilla DVC_REMOTE: gcs MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI} MLFLOW_ENABLE_PROXY_MULTIPART_UPLOAD: "true" MLFLOW_MULTIPART_UPLOAD_CHUNK_SIZE: "10485760" GIT_PYTHON_REFRESH: quiet MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST: "1" PREFECT_DISABLED: "1" SPLITS_DIR: /app/configs/splits CHECKPOINT_DIR: /app/checkpoints LOGS_DIR: /app/logs PYTORCH_ALLOC_CONF: "expandable_segments:True" HF_TOKEN: ${HF_TOKEN}
task.expected-setup-block: dvc init --no-scm -f dvc remote add gcs gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data # ADC via service account (GCP VM gets this automatically — no key files needed) dvc pull -r gcs
task.acceptance: smoke_test_gcp.yaml has: - DVC_REMOTE: gcs (not upcloud) - MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI} (not ${MLFLOW_GCP_URI}) - setup: uses "dvc remote add gcs gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data" - NO DVC_S3_* vars anywhere in the file

task.name: Verify smoke_test_mamba.yaml (already on test/mambavesselnet)
task.description: smoke_test_mamba.yaml was created on test/mambavesselnet branch. Verify it is correctly merged/available on this branch (qa/gcp-runpod-3rd-pass). It should use RunPod architecture (Network Volume, file-based MLflow).

commands.cmd: python3 -c " from pathlib import Path import yaml # SkyPilot YAMLs are valid YAML content = Path('deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_mamba.yaml').read_text(encoding='utf-8') # Verify RunPod architecture (no UpCloud) banned = ['DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL', 'upcloud', 'MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI'] for b in banned: if b in content: print(f'FAIL: {b} found in smoke_test_mamba.yaml') else: print(f'OK: {b} absent from smoke_test_mamba.yaml') # Verify Network Volume data source assert '/opt/vol' in content, 'FAIL: /opt/vol missing from mamba YAML' print('OK: Network Volume architecture confirmed') print('OK: smoke_test_mamba.yaml looks correct') "

task.acceptance: smoke_test_mamba.yaml exists, has Network Volume data, no UpCloud refs.

task.name: Commit YAML fixes to qa/gcp-runpod-3rd-pass branch

commands.cmd:
git commit -m "fix(gcp): use GCS DVC remote, direct MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI — remove UpCloud S3
Root cause of all GCP job failures (jobs 6-14 in 2026-03-16 QA session). GCP VMs authenticate to GCS via ADC (service account), not S3 keys.
Changes: - Remove DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL, DVC_S3_ACCESS_KEY, DVC_S3_SECRET_KEY, DVC_S3_BUCKET - Add DVC_REMOTE: gcs - setup: dvc remote add gcs gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data + dvc pull -r gcs - Use MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: \${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI} (not \${MLFLOW_GCP_URI} alias)
Metalearning: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-16-gcp-uses-gcs-not-upcloud-s3.md Issue: #747
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"

task.acceptance: git log shows commit with correct message. No DVC_S3_* in YAML.

phase.name: GCP 4-Model Training on L4 Spot
phase.description:
CORRECTED LAUNCH PATTERN (v2 had missing set -a):
set -a && source .env && set +a sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gcp.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_vanilla -y
OR via Makefile: make smoke-test-gcp MODEL=sam3_vanilla
TIMING EXPECTATIONS (from 2026-03-16 real data): VM provisioning + Docker pull: ~15 min (dominates cost) DVC pull from GCS: ~2 min 2-epoch training: ~9 min Total: ~26 min = $0.095 at $0.22/hr L4 spot
BETWEEN EACH MODEL: verify FINISHED + finite val_loss before launching next. IF val_loss = NaN for SAM3: check mixed_precision=False was applied.

task.name: Train sam3_vanilla on GCP L4 spot

commands.cmd: gcloud compute instances list --project=minivess-mlops # verify 0 instances

task.verification: Monitor with: sky jobs logs <job-id> Look for: "Training completed", finite val_loss in MLflow Check MLflow: curl ${MLFLOW_GCP_URI}/api/2.0/mlflow/runs/search
task.critical-checks: 1. Instance type = L4 (NOT T4 — T4 banned for SAM3) 2. Docker image pulled from GAR (not GHCR — same-region = fast) 3. DVC data pulled from GCS (NOT UpCloud S3) 4. Training FINISHED (not FAILED) 5. val_loss is finite (not NaN, not Inf) 6. MLflow run created in Cloud Run server 7. VM auto-destroyed after completion
task.ralph-loop: Monitor. On failure: capture logs BEFORE auto-destroy, diagnose, fix, retry.

task.name: Train dynunet on GCP L4 spot
task.description: DynUNet: pure MONAI model, no frozen encoder, no BF16 constraint. Should run cleanly. val_interval=1 for cloud runs.

task.name: Train sam3_hybrid on GCP L4 spot
task.description:
CRITICAL: sam3_hybrid is the model that historically produced NaN val_loss. Root causes (both fixed in smoke_test_gcp.yaml): 1. mixed_precision must be False (AMP + sliding_window = NaN, MONAI #4243) 2. val_interval must be 1 (validate every epoch, not sentinel)
The GCP YAML run: block applies ++mixed_precision=false,++val_interval=1 for all sam3 models via CLOUD_OVERRIDES.
Verify in MLflow after run: - mixed_precision param = False - val_loss column has finite values (not NaN) - max_epochs = 2, val_interval = 1
task.acceptance: val_loss is finite. This is THE validation target of this entire QA effort.

task.name: Train vesselfm on GCP L4 spot
task.description: VesselFM: requires patch Z≥32 (5 stride-2 downsamplings). L4 has 24 GB VRAM — sufficient for VesselFM (~10 GB VRAM). HF_TOKEN required for weight download (set in envs block). Previous failure was SSH error (transient) — this MUST pass on GCP.

task.name: Verify ALL 4 GCP models in Cloud Run MLflow

commands.cmd:
python3 - <<'PYEOF' import json, sys, os, urllib.request
mlflow_url = os.environ["MLFLOW_GCP_URI"].rstrip("/")
# Search all experiments req = urllib.request.Request( f"{mlflow_url}/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search", data=json.dumps({"max_results": 50}).encode(), headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}, method="POST" ) with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as r: exps = json.loads(r.read())
print(f"Found {len(exps.get('experiments', []))} experiments")
failures = [] for exp in exps.get("experiments", []): if "smoke_test" not in exp["name"]: continue # Search runs in this experiment runs_req = urllib.request.Request( f"{mlflow_url}/api/2.0/mlflow/runs/search", data=json.dumps({"experiment_ids": [exp["experiment_id"]]}).encode(), headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}, method="POST" ) with urllib.request.urlopen(runs_req) as r: runs_data = json.loads(r.read()) for run in runs_data.get("runs", []): info = run["info"] metrics = {m["key"]: m["value"] for m in run.get("data", {}).get("metrics", [])} status = info.get("status", "UNKNOWN") val_loss = metrics.get("val_loss", float("nan")) import math if status != "FINISHED" or math.isnan(val_loss) or math.isinf(val_loss): failures.append(f"FAIL: exp={exp['name']} run={info['run_id'][:8]} status={status} val_loss={val_loss}") else: print(f" OK: {exp['name']} val_loss={val_loss:.4f}")
if failures: print("FAILURES:") for f in failures: print(f" {f}") sys.exit(1) else: print("ALL models PASSED: FINISHED + finite val_loss") PYEOF

task.acceptance: 4/4 models: FINISHED status, finite val_loss, visible in Cloud Run MLflow. sam3_topolora excluded (needs A100 — hardware limitation, not a system bug).

phase.name: RunPod Dev Workflow — Network Volume + File-based MLflow
phase.description:
CORRECTED ARCHITECTURE (v2 had wrong UpCloud references):
RunPod = EPHEMERAL DEV COMPUTE for academic paper. Design: - Data from Network Volume /opt/vol/data/raw/ (pre-loaded via make dev-gpu-upload-data) - MLflow file-based at /opt/vol/mlruns (NO remote server) - Post-run sync: sky rsync down minivess-dev:/opt/vol/mlruns/ mlruns/ - Makefile target: make dev-gpu-sync
KEY DIFFERENCE FROM GCP: - RunPod syncs EVERYTHING to local (checkpoints + mlruns + .onnx) - GCP researchers use Cloud Run MLflow UI directly (never download locally)
REQUIRED ENV VARS FOR RUNPOD: - RUNPOD_API_KEY (SkyPilot auth) - GITHUB_TOKEN (GHCR private image pull) - HF_TOKEN (VesselFM weights only) - NO DVC_S3_* vars (not needed — Network Volume data) - NO MLFLOW_CLOUD_* vars (not needed — file-based MLflow)
MODELS TO TEST: 1. mambavesselnet (NEW — smoke_test_mamba.yaml from test/mambavesselnet) 2. sam3_vanilla (regression check)

task.name: Verify RunPod Network Volume has training data
task.description: CORRECTED: v2 checked UpCloud S3. RunPod uses Network Volume. If data not on volume: make dev-gpu-upload-data (rsync local data/ → /opt/vol/data/). This is a ONE-TIME setup. Network Volume has 7-day TTL.

commands.cmd: # Check if dev cluster is running (from previous session) uv run sky status 2>&1 | grep -E "(minivess-dev|UP|DOWN|INIT)"

# If dev cluster UP: verify data on volume uv run sky exec minivess-dev -- \ "ls /opt/vol/data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l" \ 2>/dev/null || echo "Dev cluster not running — will be verified post-launch"

task.on-failure: If Network Volume has no data: make dev-gpu-upload-data # rsync local data/ → /opt/vol/data/ # This requires dev cluster to be running (sky launch smoke_test_gpu.yaml)

task.name: Train mambavesselnet on RunPod (NEW model QA)
task.description: MambaVesselNet is the new model added in test/mambavesselnet branch. smoke_test_mamba.yaml uses Network Volume + file-based MLflow (CORRECT). EXPERIMENT computed in run: block (NOT envs: — SkyPilot intra-envs limitation).
task.verification: Monitor: sky jobs logs <job-id> Verify Network Volume data used (not DVC pull from external) Verify MLflow stored at /opt/vol/mlruns (not remote server) Verify FINISHED + finite val_loss
task.ralph-loop: Monitor. Diagnose if FAILED_SETUP (likely data missing from volume).

task.name: Train sam3_vanilla on RunPod (regression check)
Sync RunPod artifacts to local machine
task.description: CORRECTED from v2 T5.3 (which referenced UpCloud artifacts). RunPod artifacts live on Network Volume. Sync back to local using: make dev-gpu-sync (= sky rsync down minivess-dev:/opt/vol/mlruns/ mlruns/) ALL artifacts including .pth checkpoints — no excludes.

commands.cmd: make dev-gpu-sync # or: sky rsync down minivess-dev:/opt/vol/mlruns/ mlruns/runpod/
find mlruns/runpod/ -name "meta.yaml" | wc -l # count completed runs

task.acceptance: mlruns/runpod/ has experiment data INCLUDING .pth checkpoint files. At least 2 runs (mambavesselnet + sam3_vanilla) with FINISHED status.

task.name: Verify RunPod MLflow runs locally
task.description: CORRECTED from v2 T5.4 (which checked UpCloud MLflow). After sync, query the synced local mlruns directly.

commands.cmd:
MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=mlruns/runpod uv run python3 - <<'PYEOF' import mlflow import math
client = mlflow.MlflowClient(tracking_uri="mlruns/runpod") experiments = client.search_experiments() print(f"Found {len(experiments)} experiments in synced RunPod mlruns")
failures = [] for exp in experiments: runs = client.search_runs([exp.experiment_id]) for run in runs: status = run.info.status val_loss = run.data.metrics.get("val_loss", float("nan")) if status != "FINISHED" or math.isnan(val_loss): failures.append(f"FAIL: {exp.name} run={run.info.run_id[:8]} status={status} val_loss={val_loss}") else: print(f" OK: {exp.name} val_loss={val_loss:.4f}")
if failures: for f in failures: print(f) raise SystemExit(1) print("All RunPod runs: FINISHED + finite val_loss") PYEOF

task.acceptance: All synced RunPod runs: FINISHED + finite val_loss.

task.name: Teardown RunPod ephemeral compute
task.description: RunPod = ephemeral. Network Volume persists 7 days idle. After sync, destroy the VM to stop billing. DO NOT destroy the Network Volume — reuse on next launch.

commands.cmd: uv run sky status # verify 0 clusters

task.note: Network Volume with training data + mlruns persists for 7 days idle. SAM3/MambaVesselNet weights on volume will be re-used on next launch. Training artifacts are already on local machine (T3.4 sync).

phase.name: GCP Artifact Verification (Cloud Run MLflow Only)
phase.description:
GCP design: MLflow Cloud Run is ONLINE 24/7 for the research team. Researchers access experiments via browser — NEVER download mlruns locally. Local download is a backup/migration tool only (see issue #735).
CORRECTED from v2: v2 mentioned "UpCloud MLflow artifacts" — WRONG. GCP artifacts live in GCS (gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts/). MLflow Cloud Run serves them via signed GCS URLs.

task.name: Verify all experiments visible in MLflow Web UI

commands.cmd: python3 -c " import json, os, urllib.request mlflow_url = os.environ['MLFLOW_GCP_URI'].rstrip('/') req = urllib.request.Request( mlflow_url + '/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search', data=json.dumps({'max_results': 50}).encode(), headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, method='POST' ) with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as r: d = json.loads(r.read()) exps = d.get('experiments', []) print(f'{len(exps)} experiments in Cloud Run MLflow:') for e in exps: print(f' {e[\"name\"]}') "

task.acceptance: All 4 GCP model experiments (sam3_vanilla, dynunet, sam3_hybrid, vesselfm) visible.

task.name: Verify GCS artifacts (checkpoints + configs)

commands.cmd: gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/ | head -10 || \ echo "Note: checkpoints may be in mlflow-artifacts bucket"

task.acceptance: GCS shows checkpoint and config artifacts from all 4 model runs.

task.name: Write cloud architecture enforcement tests
task.description: Tests that verify the correct data source and MLflow per provider. These run in staging tier (fast, no GPU, no actual cloud launch).

tdd-spec.red:
test_gcp_yaml_has_no_upcloud_s3_vars: Parse smoke_test_gcp.yaml with yaml.safe_load(). Assert no DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL, DVC_S3_ACCESS_KEY, DVC_S3_SECRET_KEY in envs. Assert DVC_REMOTE == "gcs". Assert "gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data" in setup block.
test_runpod_yaml_has_no_upcloud_refs: Parse smoke_test_gpu.yaml with yaml.safe_load(). Assert MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI starts with "/opt/vol". Assert "/opt/vol/data/raw" in setup block. Assert "s3://minivessdataset" in setup block (public fallback). Assert no MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI or MLFLOW_CLOUD_PASSWORD in envs.
test_gcp_yaml_has_no_skypilot_intra_envs_resolution: Parse smoke_test_gcp.yaml with yaml.safe_load(). envs = parsed["envs"] Assert envs["MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI"] == "${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}" (NOT "${MLFLOW_GCP_URI}" — can't chain refs in SkyPilot envs block)
test_experiment_computed_in_run_not_envs: For all smoke_test_*.yaml: envs block must NOT contain EXPERIMENT key. run block must contain "EXPERIMENT=" shell assignment.
test_all_skypilot_yamls_have_image_id_docker: All deployment/skypilot/*.yaml MUST have image_id: docker:* (no bare VM).
test_gcp_yaml_uses_l4_not_t4: Parse smoke_test_gcp.yaml. accelerators dict must contain L4. accelerators dict must NOT contain T4 (banned — no BF16).

task.name: Write MLflow post-run verification tests

tdd-spec.red:
test_gcp_mlflow_health: requests.get(MLFLOW_GCP_URI + "/health") → 200. Mark with @pytest.mark.integration (requires live Cloud Run).
test_gcp_smoke_runs_finished_with_finite_metrics: Query Cloud Run MLflow for smoke_test_* experiments. For each FINISHED run: val_loss must not be NaN or Inf. Mark with @pytest.mark.integration.
test_runpod_synced_runs_have_checkpoints: If mlruns/runpod/ exists: verify .pth files present. Mark with @pytest.mark.integration.

task.name: Update CLAUDE.md with corrected architecture summary
task.description: Add explicit data source and MLflow section to Cloud GPU Strategy table. Ensure T4 ban, GCS-for-GCP, Network-Volume-for-RunPod are documented there. (Complements memory/feedback_gcp_data_source.md which is already written.)
task.note: Add to Cloud GPU Strategy table a "Data Source" and "MLflow" column. RunPod: Data=Network Volume /opt/vol, MLflow=file-based /opt/vol/mlruns GCP: Data=GCS gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data (ADC), MLflow=Cloud Run

phase.name: FinOps Sprint 1 — Timing Sentinel + Cost Tracker
phase.description:
From 2026-03-16 real GCP data: - Docker pull from GAR dominates: ~15 min (58% of smoke-test cost) - At $0.22/hr L4 spot: setup costs $0.055/trial - ASHA min_resource=3 → 62% overhead per HPO trial - Recommended: ASHA min_resource=13 for GCP HPO
Sprint 1 implements MEASUREMENT (not optimization). Optimization (image size reduction, cached machine images) is Sprint 2-3. Full plan: docs/planning/profiler-benchmarking-plan-triple-check.md

task.name: Add timing sentinel to SkyPilot YAML setup/run blocks
task.description: Add echo statements capturing phase timestamps in smoke_test_gcp.yaml and smoke_test_gpu.yaml. These stdout lines will be parsed by infra_timing.py. Format: "TIMING: phase=<name> ts=<unix_timestamp>" Phases: vm_ready, docker_ready, dvc_done, training_start, training_end NEVER use regex to parse (CLAUDE.md Rule #16) — emit as key=value pairs.

tdd-spec.red: test_gcp_yaml_has_timing_sentinels: Parse smoke_test_gcp.yaml. setup block must contain: "TIMING: phase=docker_ready ts=" (captures when container is running) "TIMING: phase=dvc_done ts=" (captures after dvc pull -r gcs) run block must contain: "TIMING: phase=training_start ts=" "TIMING: phase=training_end ts="

task.name: Implement infra_timing.py log parser

tdd-spec.red: test_parse_timing_sentinels_from_log_lines: Lines = ["TIMING: phase=docker_ready ts=1742000000", "TIMING: phase=dvc_done ts=1742000900", "TIMING: phase=training_start ts=1742001020", "TIMING: phase=training_end ts=1742001560"] result = parse_timing_lines(lines) # result["docker_pull_seconds"] == 900 (dvc_done - docker_ready assumed = pull time) # result["dvc_pull_seconds"] == 120 # result["training_seconds"] == 540 NO REGEX — use str.split() and str.partition() only.

task.name: Implement cost_tracker.py

tdd-spec.red: test_compute_job_cost: timing = { "docker_pull_seconds": 900, "dvc_pull_seconds": 120, "training_seconds": 540, "total_wall_seconds": 1560, } rates = {"gcp_l4_spot": 0.22} result = compute_job_cost(timing, "gcp_l4_spot", rates) assert abs(result["total_cost_usd"] - 0.22 * (1560/3600)) < 0.001 assert abs(result["setup_overhead_fraction"] - (1020/1560)) < 0.01 assert result["gpu_utilization"] == training_seconds / total_wall_seconds

task.name: Log timing + cost to MLflow as setup_* / cost_* params
task.description: Integrate infra_timing.py + cost_tracker.py into train_flow.py. Log as MLflow params (not metrics) so they appear in comparison tables. Prefix: setup_* (timing), cost_* (financial). Examples: setup_docker_pull_s, setup_dvc_pull_s, setup_training_s, cost_total_usd, cost_setup_overhead_fraction, cost_gpu_utilization

task.name: Write QA verification report
task.description: Document: every model trained, every artifact verified, every YAML corrected. Include: MLflow experiment IDs, GCS bucket contents, timing data (real values), costs (calculated from timing), corrections made from v1/v2 plans. Cross-reference issue #747 for FinOps follow-up.

task.name: Merge qa/gcp-runpod-3rd-pass to test/mambavesselnet

commands.cmd:
git merge --no-ff qa/gcp-runpod-3rd-pass -m "feat: GCP GCS data source fix + RunPod architecture corrections + FinOps Sprint 1
Corrections applied: - smoke_test_gcp.yaml: GCS DVC remote (not UpCloud S3) - smoke_test_gcp.yaml: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI direct (not alias) - Cloud architecture enforcement tests added - FinOps timing sentinel + cost_tracker.py (Sprint 1)
Architecture now correct: - GCP: GCS data (ADC auth) + Cloud Run MLflow - RunPod: Network Volume data + file-based MLflow on /opt/vol/mlruns
See: docs/planning/remaining-runpod-gcp-qa-3rd-pass.xml .claude/metalearning/2026-03-16-gcp-uses-gcs-not-upcloud-s3.md Issue #747
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"

task.name: Cost control — GCP infrastructure decision
task.description: Cloud SQL costs ~$0.80/day. Cloud Run MLflow costs ~$0.10/day (scale to zero). DECIDE: keep running (for continued development) or pulumi destroy. If keeping: confirm GCP budget alert is set at $20/month. Run: gcloud billing budgets list --billing-account=ACCOUNT
task.note: RunPod: already destroyed in P3.6. Network Volume auto-deletes after 7 days idle. GCP: Cloud SQL is the main ongoing cost driver.

cost-summary.item: 4 GCP models × $0.095 each on L4 spot ($0.22/hr)
RunPod mamba + sam3_vanilla on RTX 4090 ($0.44/hr × ~1.5 hr)
GCP artifact verification via Cloud Run API
cost-summary.total: ~$1-2 training costs + ongoing GCP infra ~$0.90/day if not destroyed
cost-summary.note: v2 estimate was $5-10 because it included Pulumi rebuild ($2-5/day). v3 is MUCH CHEAPER because GCP infra is ALREADY UP from previous session.

pattern.name: FAILED_SETUP: dvc pull fails on GCP
pattern.symptom: DVC pull error, no credentials for GCS
pattern.root-cause: ADC not configured for GCP VM service account
pattern.fix: 1. Verify Pulumi IAM: service account has roles/storage.objectViewer on GCS bucket 2. Verify GCS bucket is populated: gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/ | wc -l 3. If empty: dvc push -r gcs from local (one-time)

pattern.name: FAILED: val_loss=NaN for SAM3 on GCP
pattern.symptom: val_loss is NaN in MLflow after training completes
pattern.root-cause: Either: mixed_precision=True (AMP + sliding_window = NaN, MONAI #4243) Or: val_interval sentinel applied (validation skipped, then NaN logged)
pattern.fix: Verify in MLflow params: mixed_precision=false, val_interval=1 Check CLOUD_OVERRIDES in smoke_test_gcp.yaml run: block Expected HYDRA_OVERRIDES: "...++mixed_precision=false,++val_interval=1"

pattern.name: FAILED_SETUP: SkyPilot env var is literal ${VAR}
pattern.root-cause: Missing "set -a" before source .env, OR var not in .env
pattern.fix: Launch command: set -a && source .env && set +a && sky jobs launch ... Verify var is in .env: grep MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI .env

pattern.name: FAILED_SETUP: Network Volume data missing (RunPod)
pattern.symptom: Training data missing — DVC pull failed in setup
pattern.root-cause: Network Volume not populated (first use after 7-day TTL expiry)
pattern.fix: make dev-gpu-upload-data # rsync local data/ to /opt/vol/data/ # Or: dvc pull -r remote_storage (from AWS S3 public fallback)

pattern.name: FAILED: Docker image pull timeout
pattern.root-cause: GHCR: private package, GITHUB_TOKEN not set or invalid GAR: gcloud auth configure-docker not done for RunPod Both: 9 GB image takes 15-20 min on first pull (normal — not a failure)
pattern.fix: GCP (GAR): same-region pull — fast. If fail: gcloud auth configure-docker europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev RunPod (GHCR): make the GHCR package public, OR set GITHUB_TOKEN in .env Image size reduction: see Sprint 2 in profiler-benchmarking-plan-triple-check.md

provider.name: GCP
provider.data: GCS gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data via DVC (ADC auth — no S3 keys)
provider.gpu: L4 (Ada Lovelace, BF16) — T4 BANNED (no BF16, NaN in SAM3)

provider.name: RunPod
provider.mlflow-sync: make dev-gpu-sync (= sky rsync down minivess-dev:/opt/vol/mlruns/ mlruns/)

execution-notes.note: P0 is verification only — DO NOT destroy working GCP infrastructure.
P1 YAML fix is the critical prerequisite for ALL GCP training (P2).
GCP models MUST run sequentially (quota = 1 GPU).
RunPod and GCP phases can run in parallel (different cloud providers).
sam3_topolora excluded from smoke tests (needs A100 — hardware, not system bug).
val_loss=NaN for sam3_hybrid is THE acceptance criterion of this entire QA effort.
FinOps Sprint 1 (P6) = measurement only. Do NOT optimize yet (Sprint 2-3 in separate issue).
After P7: DECIDE to keep or destroy GCP Cloud SQL (~$0.80/day ongoing cost).
SOURCING PATTERN (required for SkyPilot launches): set -a && source .env && set +a && sky jobs launch ... WITHOUT set -a: vars not exported → SkyPilot sees literal "${VAR}" strings.
SkyPilot YAML env var syntax: ONLY ${VAR} supported. BANNED: ${VAR:-default} (bash fallback not supported by os.path.expandvars()).

Cross-references: - docs/planning/profiler-benchmarking-plan-triple-check.md (FinOps timing plan) - .claude/metalearning/2026-03-16-gcp-uses-gcs-not-upcloud-s3.md (architecture fix) - memory/feedback_gcp_data_source.md (per-cloud data source mandate) - GitHub Issue #747 (P1 FinOps tracking) - deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml (correct RunPod reference implementation)
